brief: One legible as-built drawing of our real-time trust-and-safety moderation stack: reports and the content firehose in, classifier serving with its model registry, the policy engine deciding, the enforcement log, quality watch, and the human review queue — with the review queue named honestly as the capacity constraint.

arguments.contentBrief: One legible as-built drawing of our real-time trust-and-safety moderation stack: reports and the content firehose in, classifier serving with its model registry, the policy engine deciding, the enforcement log, quality watch, and the human review queue — with the review queue named honestly as the capacity constraint.

structuredContent.rationale: Selected 'drawing-office' (exp-system-architecture, art-directed style); shares audience(s) [technical, mixed]; matched intent keyword(s) [onboard, new, engineers, support, architecture, review, built]; art-directed style fits standard corporate suitability; motion preference 2 noted (the template locks its own motion).

sections.purpose: The sheet identity — the drafting chrome, the title block, its revision table, and the required provenance notice.

spec.name: sheet.pageTitle
spec.guidance: The browser-tab title (assigned in JS, derived byte-identically from the fill), e.g. "The Drawing Office — Model Decision Platform, As Built".

spec.guidance: The drawing-office name on the top chrome, e.g. "THE DRAWING OFFICE".

spec.guidance: One-to-six revision rows (rev, date, description, by), oldest first, e.g. { rev: "A", date: "2026-07-04", description: "ISSUED AS BUILT", by: "MO" }.

sections.purpose: The editorial hero — the kicker, the multi-line display statement, and the one-line lede.

spec.name: masthead.kicker
spec.guidance: The eyebrow above the display statement, e.g. "AS-BUILT RECORD · CHECKED AND SIGNED".

slots.guidance: The eyebrow above the display statement, e.g. "AS-BUILT RECORD · CHECKED AND SIGNED".

spec.name: masthead.displayLines
spec.guidance: One-to-three display lines (one per array entry, rendered as broken lines), e.g. "An architecture is a promise written down.".

slots.guidance: One-to-three display lines (one per array entry, rendered as broken lines), e.g. "An architecture is a promise written down.".
slots.example: An architecture is a promise written down.

spec.name: masthead.lede
spec.guidance: The lede tail; the template prefixes the sheet title ("{title} — {lede}"), e.g. "the decision fabric behind ninety million daily answers, drawn the way it actually runs.".

slots.guidance: The lede tail; the template prefixes the sheet title ("{title} — {lede}"), e.g. "the decision fabric behind ninety million daily answers, drawn the way it actually runs.".

sections.purpose: The general-arrangement drawing — the drawn parts, their connections, the zone boxes and dimension lines. The plan placement and routing are template-fixed; the fill carries the content and pins the id sets.

spec.name: drawing.figureHeading
spec.guidance: The visually-hidden heading of the sheet region, e.g. "The general-arrangement drawing".

slots.guidance: The visually-hidden heading of the sheet region, e.g. "The general-arrangement drawing".

spec.name: drawing.caption
spec.guidance: The full accessible caption of the aria-hidden drawing, naming the parts, zones, and pointing to the schedule, e.g. "General-arrangement drawing of the model decision platform: ten parts across a channel DMZ, a restricted core zone, and an overnight oversight band…".

slots.guidance: The full accessible caption of the aria-hidden drawing, naming the parts, zones, and pointing to the schedule, e.g. "General-arrangement drawing of the model decision platform: ten parts across a channel DMZ, a restricted core zone, and an overnight oversight band…".
slots.example: General-arrangement drawing of the model decision platform: ten parts across a channel DMZ, a restricted core zone, and an overnight oversight band…

spec.name: drawing.constraintFlag
spec.guidance: The short flag label drawn beside the constrained part, e.g. "NOTE 3".

slots.guidance: The short flag label drawn beside the constrained part, e.g. "NOTE 3".

spec.name: drawing.nodes
spec.guidance: Exactly ten parts. FIXED-SLOT: ids must be exactly the shipped plan set (edge-gateway, decision-api, stream-ingest, feature-store, model-serving, policy-engine, decision-log, drift-watch, model-registry, case-review) — the template's hand-drafted placement is keyed by id. Each: id, label, kind (start|process|data|decision|end), emphasis (standard|constrained). Exactly ONE part is "constrained" — the hatched capacity constraint, e.g. { id: "feature-store", label: "FEATURE STORE", kind: "data", emphasis: "constrained" }.

slots.guidance: Exactly ten parts. FIXED-SLOT: ids must be exactly the shipped plan set (edge-gateway, decision-api, stream-ingest, feature-store, model-serving, policy-engine, decision-log, drift-watch, model-registry, case-review) — the template's hand-drafted placement is keyed by id. Each: id, label, kind (start|process|data|decision|end), emphasis (standard|constrained). Exactly ONE part is "constrained" — the hatched capacity constraint, e.g. { id: "feature-store", label: "FEATURE STORE", kind: "data", emphasis: "constrained" }.

spec.name: drawing.edges
spec.guidance: Exactly ten connections. FIXED-SLOT: ids must be exactly the shipped routing set (e-edge-api, e-edge-ingest, e-ingest-fs, e-api-serving, e-fs-serving, e-registry-serving, e-serving-policy, e-policy-log, e-log-drift, e-drift-case). Each: id, from (node id), to (node id), label, e.g. { id: "e-fs-serving", from: "feature-store", to: "model-serving", label: "≤ 12 ms p50 read" }.

slots.guidance: Exactly ten connections. FIXED-SLOT: ids must be exactly the shipped routing set (e-edge-api, e-edge-ingest, e-ingest-fs, e-api-serving, e-fs-serving, e-registry-serving, e-serving-policy, e-policy-log, e-log-drift, e-drift-case). Each: id, from (node id), to (node id), label, e.g. { id: "e-fs-serving", from: "feature-store", to: "model-serving", label: "≤ 12 ms p50 read" }.

spec.name: drawing.zones
spec.guidance: Exactly three zone boxes (id, label). FIXED-SLOT ids (channel-dmz, core-zone, oversight); geometry is template-fixed, e.g. { id: "core-zone", label: "CORE ZONE — RESTRICTED · NOTE 1" }.

slots.guidance: Exactly three zone boxes (id, label). FIXED-SLOT ids (channel-dmz, core-zone, oversight); geometry is template-fixed, e.g. { id: "core-zone", label: "CORE ZONE — RESTRICTED · NOTE 1" }.

spec.name: drawing.dimensions
spec.guidance: Exactly two dimension lines (id, label). FIXED-SLOT ids (hot-path, durable-record); geometry is template-fixed, e.g. { id: "hot-path", label: "HOT PATH TRAVERSE ≤ 180 MS P99 · EDGE TO ANSWER" }.

slots.guidance: Exactly two dimension lines (id, label). FIXED-SLOT ids (hot-path, durable-record); geometry is template-fixed, e.g. { id: "hot-path", label: "HOT PATH TRAVERSE ≤ 180 MS P99 · EDGE TO ANSWER" }.

sections.purpose: The general notes and the drawing legend — the constraint note is the single critical entry.

spec.name: notes.items
spec.guidance: Two-to-eight numbered general notes (no, text, optional critical flag). Mark the capacity-constraint note critical, e.g. { no: 3, text: "FEATURE-STORE READ REPLICAS AT 78% OF RATED THROUGHPUT…", critical: true }.

slots.guidance: Two-to-eight numbered general notes (no, text, optional critical flag). Mark the capacity-constraint note critical, e.g. { no: 3, text: "FEATURE-STORE READ REPLICAS AT 78% OF RATED THROUGHPUT…", critical: true }.

spec.name: notes.legend
spec.guidance: The drawing legend line explaining the encoding, e.g. "LEGEND — DOUBLE LINE: PART · DASH: ZONE BOUNDARY · HATCH: CONSTRAINED PART · A–A / B–B: SECTION CUTS · DIM LINES CARRY LATENCY BUDGETS".

slots.guidance: The drawing legend line explaining the encoding, e.g. "LEGEND — DOUBLE LINE: PART · DASH: ZONE BOUNDARY · HATCH: CONSTRAINED PART · A–A / B–B: SECTION CUTS · DIM LINES CARRY LATENCY BUDGETS".
slots.example: LEGEND — DOUBLE LINE: PART · DASH: ZONE BOUNDARY · HATCH: CONSTRAINED PART · A–A / B–B: SECTION CUTS · DIM LINES CARRY LATENCY BUDGETS

sections.purpose: The measured editorial narrative — numbered sections with mono margin annotations.

spec.name: narrative.sections
spec.guidance: Three-to-seven narrative sections (no, title, annotation, paragraphs 1–3, hostsFigure). Exactly ONE section carries hostsFigure: true — the capacity figure renders inside it (structural, not tied to the section number), e.g. { no: "04", title: "Capacity", annotation: "NOTE 3 · CONSTRAINT — SEE FIG 4.1", paragraphs: ["One component on this sheet is drawn with a hatch…"], hostsFigure: true }.

slots.guidance: Three-to-seven narrative sections (no, title, annotation, paragraphs 1–3, hostsFigure). Exactly ONE section carries hostsFigure: true — the capacity figure renders inside it (structural, not tied to the section number), e.g. { no: "04", title: "Capacity", annotation: "NOTE 3 · CONSTRAINT — SEE FIG 4.1", paragraphs: ["One component on this sheet is drawn with a hatch…"], hostsFigure: true }.

sections.purpose: FIG 4.1 — the capacity-headroom bar chart embedded in the capacity section; the constraint tier draws below the floor.

spec.name: figure.number
spec.guidance: The figure number, e.g. "FIG 4.1".

slots.guidance: The figure number, e.g. "FIG 4.1".

spec.name: figure.title
spec.guidance: The figure title, e.g. "CAPACITY HEADROOM BY COMPONENT".

slots.guidance: The figure title, e.g. "CAPACITY HEADROOM BY COMPONENT".

spec.name: figure.chartTitle
spec.guidance: The chart's accessible title (assigned via prop), e.g. "FIG 4.1 — Capacity headroom by component".

slots.guidance: The chart's accessible title (assigned via prop), e.g. "FIG 4.1 — Capacity headroom by component".

spec.name: figure.caption
spec.guidance: The figure caption; state synthetic provenance and the floor, e.g. "Remaining throughput headroom, percent of rated capacity… Diamond marks the 25% floor…".

slots.guidance: The figure caption; state synthetic provenance and the floor, e.g. "Remaining throughput headroom, percent of rated capacity… Diamond marks the 25% floor…".
slots.example: Remaining throughput headroom, percent of rated capacity… Diamond marks the 25% floor…

spec.name: figure.source
spec.guidance: The chart source note; state synthetic provenance, e.g. "SOURCE: CAPACITY REVIEW 2026-06 · SYNTHETIC DEMONSTRATION DATA".

slots.guidance: The chart source note; state synthetic provenance, e.g. "SOURCE: CAPACITY REVIEW 2026-06 · SYNTHETIC DEMONSTRATION DATA".
slots.example: SOURCE: CAPACITY REVIEW 2026-06 · SYNTHETIC DEMONSTRATION DATA

spec.name: figure.floorPct
spec.guidance: The headroom floor the review board requires, percent 0–100; tiers below it draw in the red ink, e.g. 25.

slots.guidance: The headroom floor the review board requires, percent 0–100; tiers below it draw in the red ink, e.g. 25.

spec.name: figure.headroom
spec.guidance: Four-to-eight capacity tiers (id, category, value percent 0–100, target = floorPct). Tiers whose value is below floorPct are the constraint, e.g. { category: "Feature store (read)", value: 22, target: 25 }.

slots.guidance: Four-to-eight capacity tiers (id, category, value percent 0–100, target = floorPct). Tiers whose value is below floorPct are the constraint, e.g. { category: "Feature store (read)", value: 22, target: 25 }.

craftGuarantees: A platform architecture staged as a SIGNED ENGINEERING DRAWING: the commanding visual is a hand-drafted general-arrangement plan — parts as double-lined boxes on a drafting-grid field, orthogonally routed connections, dimension lines carrying latency budgets, section cuts, zone boundaries, and a proper title block with a revision table.

THE LAYOUT IS FIXED-SLOT. The plan placement of every part, the routing of every connection, and the position of every zone box and dimension line are hand-drafted per shipped id and owned by the template. The fill re-labels the drawing (part labels/classes, connection endpoints/labels, zone and dimension captions); the schema pins the node, edge, zone, and dimension ID SETS to exactly the shipped ids, so a fill can never add a part the drawing has nowhere to place. Any schema-valid fill renders composed.

Exactly one part carries emphasis "constrained": the single hatched, flag-marked capacity constraint — echoed as the highlighted row of the schedule of parts, the critical general note, and the sub-floor tier of FIG 4.1.

The drawing SVG is decorative (aria-hidden); the REAL content is the visible SCHEDULE OF PARTS table and CONNECTIONS list (derived from the same nodes/edges) plus the title block — so the world is fully legible without the drawing.
The synthetic-data provenance notice (sheet.dataNotice) is required and prints in the footer; the title-block classification reinforces it.

FIG 4.1 colours any tier whose headroom is below the review-board floor (figure.floorPct) in the drawing's red ink — the encoding is derived from the data, never hardcoded to a tier id. The figure's placement is STRUCTURAL: it renders inside the one narrative section flagged hostsFigure: true (the schema requires exactly one), never by matching a section number.

Slot char caps and item counts are sized so any schema-valid fill stays composed — the display statement never overflows, the schedule and notes stay balanced, and the narrative paragraphs stay legible. Motion level 2 (data-ink-draw); the mood is locked light.

slots.example: LEGEND — DOUBLE LINE: PART · DASH: ZONE BOUNDARY · HATCH: CONSTRAINED PART · A–A / B–B: SECTION CUTS · DIM LINES CARRY LATENCY BUDGETS

sections.purpose: The measured editorial narrative — numbered sections with mono margin annotations.

spec.name: narrative.sections
spec.guidance: Three-to-seven narrative sections (no, title, annotation, paragraphs 1–3, hostsFigure). Exactly ONE section carries hostsFigure: true — the capacity figure renders inside it (structural, not tied to the section number), e.g. { no: "04", title: "Capacity", annotation: "NOTE 3 · CONSTRAINT — SEE FIG 4.1", paragraphs: ["One component on this sheet is drawn with a hatch…"], hostsFigure: true }.

slots.guidance: Three-to-seven narrative sections (no, title, annotation, paragraphs 1–3, hostsFigure). Exactly ONE section carries hostsFigure: true — the capacity figure renders inside it (structural, not tied to the section number), e.g. { no: "04", title: "Capacity", annotation: "NOTE 3 · CONSTRAINT — SEE FIG 4.1", paragraphs: ["One component on this sheet is drawn with a hatch…"], hostsFigure: true }.

sections.purpose: FIG 4.1 — the capacity-headroom bar chart embedded in the capacity section; the constraint tier draws below the floor.

spec.name: figure.number
spec.guidance: The figure number, e.g. "FIG 4.1".

slots.guidance: The figure number, e.g. "FIG 4.1".

spec.name: figure.title
spec.guidance: The figure title, e.g. "CAPACITY HEADROOM BY COMPONENT".

slots.guidance: The figure title, e.g. "CAPACITY HEADROOM BY COMPONENT".

spec.name: figure.chartTitle
spec.guidance: The chart's accessible title (assigned via prop), e.g. "FIG 4.1 — Capacity headroom by component".

slots.guidance: The chart's accessible title (assigned via prop), e.g. "FIG 4.1 — Capacity headroom by component".

spec.name: figure.caption
spec.guidance: The figure caption; state synthetic provenance and the floor, e.g. "Remaining throughput headroom, percent of rated capacity… Diamond marks the 25% floor…".

slots.guidance: The figure caption; state synthetic provenance and the floor, e.g. "Remaining throughput headroom, percent of rated capacity… Diamond marks the 25% floor…".
slots.example: Remaining throughput headroom, percent of rated capacity… Diamond marks the 25% floor…

spec.name: figure.source
spec.guidance: The chart source note; state synthetic provenance, e.g. "SOURCE: CAPACITY REVIEW 2026-06 · SYNTHETIC DEMONSTRATION DATA".

slots.guidance: The chart source note; state synthetic provenance, e.g. "SOURCE: CAPACITY REVIEW 2026-06 · SYNTHETIC DEMONSTRATION DATA".
slots.example: SOURCE: CAPACITY REVIEW 2026-06 · SYNTHETIC DEMONSTRATION DATA

spec.name: figure.floorPct
spec.guidance: The headroom floor the review board requires, percent 0–100; tiers below it draw in the red ink, e.g. 25.

slots.guidance: The headroom floor the review board requires, percent 0–100; tiers below it draw in the red ink, e.g. 25.

spec.name: figure.headroom
spec.guidance: Four-to-eight capacity tiers (id, category, value percent 0–100, target = floorPct). Tiers whose value is below floorPct are the constraint, e.g. { category: "Feature store (read)", value: 22, target: 25 }.

slots.guidance: Four-to-eight capacity tiers (id, category, value percent 0–100, target = floorPct). Tiers whose value is below floorPct are the constraint, e.g. { category: "Feature store (read)", value: 22, target: 25 }.

craftGuarantees: A platform architecture staged as a SIGNED ENGINEERING DRAWING: the commanding visual is a hand-drafted general-arrangement plan — parts as double-lined boxes on a drafting-grid field, orthogonally routed connections, dimension lines carrying latency budgets, section cuts, zone boundaries, and a proper title block with a revision table.

THE LAYOUT IS FIXED-SLOT. The plan placement of every part, the routing of every connection, and the position of every zone box and dimension line are hand-drafted per shipped id and owned by the template. The fill re-labels the drawing (part labels/classes, connection endpoints/labels, zone and dimension captions); the schema pins the node, edge, zone, and dimension ID SETS to exactly the shipped ids, so a fill can never add a part the drawing has nowhere to place. Any schema-valid fill renders composed.

Exactly one part carries emphasis "constrained": the single hatched, flag-marked capacity constraint — echoed as the highlighted row of the schedule of parts, the critical general note, and the sub-floor tier of FIG 4.1.

The drawing SVG is decorative (aria-hidden); the REAL content is the visible SCHEDULE OF PARTS table and CONNECTIONS list (derived from the same nodes/edges) plus the title block — so the world is fully legible without the drawing.
The synthetic-data provenance notice (sheet.dataNotice) is required and prints in the footer; the title-block classification reinforces it.

FIG 4.1 colours any tier whose headroom is below the review-board floor (figure.floorPct) in the drawing's red ink — the encoding is derived from the data, never hardcoded to a tier id. The figure's placement is STRUCTURAL: it renders inside the one narrative section flagged hostsFigure: true (the schema requires exactly one), never by matching a section number.

Slot char caps and item counts are sized so any schema-valid fill stays composed — the display statement never overflows, the schedule and notes stay balanced, and the narrative paragraphs stay legible. Motion level 2 (data-ink-draw); the mood is locked light.
